_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Clinical notes confirming Congenital Dysplasia of Hip (CDH) / Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip (DDH)
Presence of a palpable 'clunk' with Ortolani maneuver
Hip subluxation out of the acetabulum with a 'clunk' during Barlow's test
Presence of Galeazzi sign (one leg appearing shorter than the other) in unilateral hip dislocation for late DDH (age 3-6 months)
Confirmation of DDH by Plain Radiography (standing anteroposterior view of pelvis, false profile view)

preauth.label: X-ray/Ultrasonography –both Hips confirming CDH labelled with patient ID, date.

claim.label: Post-op X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)

cpd_claim.q: Does Post-op X-ray show the corrected hip dislocation and structure of hip joint?

common_queries: Clinical notes confirming CDH are missing or incomplete
X-ray/Ultrasonography of both hips confirming CDH is missing or not labelled with patient ID and date
Post-op X-ray is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, and side (Left/Right)
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing or incomplete
Post-op X-ray does not show corrected hip dislocation or structure of hip joint

clinical_key_pointers: Congenital dislocation of the hip is also known as hip dysplasia or Congenital/developmental dysplasia of the hip (CDH/DDH). The term DDH is used in referring to patients who are born with dislocation or instability of the hip, which may then result in hip dysplasia.
